mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near zero (0.37%) continues to compress the equity discount rate, keeping the relative valuation appeal of stocks versus cash/bonds elevated and supporting a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as accommodative policy persists.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.37 as of t-1mo, near zero lower bound
regime_hint: any regime with near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above a 0.3 percentage point threshold over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede consumer demand weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: In more than 55% of triggered episodes, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the signal's rank IC is indistinguishable from zero (CI includes zero) across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth remains strong (PAYEMS rising) while the Fed simultaneously begins raising the federal funds rate, the joint signal indicates monetary tightening into an overheating labor market, raising recession and multiple-compression risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND FEDFUNDS rising for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=143915k as of t-1mo, FEDFUNDS=0.37 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: early monetary tightening cycle with tight labor market
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint trigger are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term's incremental rank IC over either single-series proxy is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion phase, elevated retail sales (RSAFS) reflecting robust consumer spending signal near-term corporate revenue strength, producing a short-lived positive S&P 500 return over the following 5 trading days as market participants extrapolate near-term earnings momentum.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong consumer spending
failure_condition: 5-day forward return following RSAFS month-over-month increase is not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p>0.05) over a 2-year rolling sample, or reverses to negative in a recession regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real economic output and manufacturing capacity utilization, supporting corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
regime_hint: any regime with expanding industrial output
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month INDPRO growth and forward 60d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or reverses sign in two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a 5% year-over-year threshold signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and forward-looking construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in slower cyclical growth.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following HOUST YoY decline below -5% are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or rank IC is not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the federal funds rate remains far below the inflation rate (deeply negative real rates), the resulting financial repression pushes investors toward equities as a store of value, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days), until the Fed responds with aggressive tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY inflation minus FEDFUNDS > 3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=237.92 as of t-2mo, FEDFUNDS=0.37 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: deeply negative real interest rate regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during negative real rate episodes are not significantly positive (p>0.05), or turn negative once real rates begin normalizing within the sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where unemployment is already low (near 5%) and payroll growth remains strong, the labor market signals overheating and imminent policy tightening risk, causing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days to underperform relative to mid-cycle periods due to rising rate-hike expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.0 as of t-1mo, PAYEMS=143915k as of t-1mo
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns in low-unemployment, strong-payroll regimes are not significantly lower than in mid-cycle regimes (p>0.05), or direction flips positive across two consecutive late-cycle episodes.
